explanation:
**Official procedures** in Argentina have costs, processing times, and requirements set by each government agency (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Immigration Office, Civil Registry, etc.). This calculator estimates your total cost and required documentation.

## How It Calculates

Temporary or permanent residency plus foreign ID costs—instant and free.

This calculator applies the formula using your input values, verifying typical ranges and alerting you to any unusually high or low values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. All values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much does temporary residency cost for foreigners in Argentina?
faq.a: Costs vary based on nationality (Mercosur vs. non-Mercosur) and residency type. Mercosur nationals pay a reduced fee, while non-Mercosur nationals pay a higher rate. This calculator shows exact costs for your situation.

faq.q: What's the difference between Mercosur and non-Mercosur residency?
faq.a: Mercosur countries (Argentina, Uruguay, Paraguay, Brazil, Chile) enjoy reduced residency fees and simplified requirements. Non-Mercosur nationals face higher fees and additional documentation requirements.

faq.q: How long does it take to get a foreign ID in Argentina?
faq.a: Standard processing takes about 15 days with all required documents. Express service is available for an additional fee. Most processes require a prior appointment booked online at argentina.gob.ar.

faq.q: Can I apply for residency online in Argentina?
faq.a: Many services are digital (AFIP, ANSES, civil records requests, address changes). However, some procedures require in-person submission (contracts, marriage registration, certain civil documents).

faq.q: What's the difference between temporary and permanent residency?
faq.a: Temporary residency is your first step. After 2-3 years of legal residence, you can apply for permanent residency, which grants longer-term stability but requires proving continuous residence.

faq.q: Do I need an appointment for immigration procedures?
faq.a: Yes, most services require an appointment. You can book online through argentina.gob.ar or the specific agency's website. Walk-ins without appointments are only handled for urgent cases.

faq.q: What documents do I need for foreign residency in Argentina?
faq.a: Requirements vary by residency type and visa category. Generally, you'll need your passport, proof of financial means, health certificate, and criminal record clearance. Specific requirements depend on your situation.
Yes, this calculator uses rates updated to April 2026. However, government fees can change. Always verify current rates with official sources (Migraciones, RENAPER) before submitting applications.
